title: botryopleuron kitamurae
intro: Botryopleuron kitamurae, also known as Japanese angelica or Kitamura's angelica, is a type of flowering plant in the carrot family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain and swelling, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions.
